module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand calculation sheets
learningObjectives.description: Explain pricing logic components and sequencing used in charge determination.

learningObjectives.title: Understand rate tables
learningObjectives.description: Describe how pricing values are stored and consumed during calculations.

learningObjectives.title: Understand sheet-table interaction
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how logic and data combine for automated settlement-ready costs.

sections.content: Charge Calculation Sheets define the logic SAP TM uses to calculate transportation charges, while Rate Tables store the actual pricing values. Together they enable automatic, consistent and contract-based freight cost calculation for transportation planning and settlement.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Rate Tables provide the numbers. Charge Calculation Sheets provide the calculation logic.

A shipment travels 850 km using a 20-ton truck. SAP TM selects the applicable Freight Agreement, reads the distance rate from the Rate Table, applies the fuel surcharge defined in the Charge Calculation Sheet and calculates the total transportation charge automatically.

sections.title: Charge Calculation Sheet vs Rate Table
Best Practice
sections.content: Separate pricing data from calculation logic. Update Rate Tables frequently while keeping Charge Calculation Sheets stable whenever possible.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics provider maintained over 500 carrier-specific Rate Tables while using only 18 standardized Charge Calculation Sheets, simplifying maintenance and reducing configuration effort.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember this certification concept: Rate Table = Pricing Values, Charge Calculation Sheet = Pricing Logic.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not store business logic inside Rate Tables. Business logic belongs in Charge Calculation Sheets; Rate Tables should contain only pricing values.

items: Charge Calculation Sheets define pricing logic.
Both are used together during charge calculation.
Freight Agreements reference Charge Calculation Sheets.
Accurate configuration ensures correct freight settlement.
